description:
## Background

Same-Workspace Back/Forward can restore renderer state directly, but an Issue Navigation Entry may belong to another Workspace. Explicit history traversal should restore that Workspace without another confirmation while preserving the atomic invariant that Workspace identity, route, and Issue Explorer snapshot cannot diverge.

Triage: `ready-for-agent`.

## What to build

Make Back and Forward traverse Issue Navigation Entries across Workspaces. A successful traversal restores Workspace, Issue List View, Issue Search, and optional Issue together. A failed traversal preserves the current visible destination and shows the global error banner.

## Technical Approach

- Extend the navigation coordinator’s popstate handling to compare the destination entry’s Workspace path with Current Workspace.
- Treat Back/Forward as explicit authorization to switch to a Workspace already recorded in the session ledger; do not show deep-link confirmation.
- Use the normal atomic Workspace-selection operation and existing transition gate. Do not publish the destination route as complete until the matching Workspace snapshot commits.
- Preserve a rollback description of the current Issue Navigation Entry before attempting the switch.
- On success, restore destination view, search, and optional Issue from identifiers only, re-resolving Issue data from the newly loaded authoritative Issue List.
- If the Issue is no longer present, retain the route and render Issue-not-found.
- If the Workspace cannot resolve/load, return the browser history position and renderer route to the preserved entry without creating a new semantic entry, retain the current snapshot, and show the global error banner.
- Prevent rollback navigation from recursively triggering another Workspace traversal or corrupting navigation indexes/forward boundaries.
- Maintain correct Back/Forward enabled states and adjacent destination tooltips after success or rollback.

## Considerations

- Browser popstate changes the URL before asynchronous Workspace loading completes. The coordinator needs an explicit pending/rollback protocol rather than allowing a mismatched destination to become visible.
- A recorded Workspace can be removed from the Workspace Catalog after the entry was created. History traversal may use the recorded canonical path through normal selection, but failure must remain atomic.
- No Issue data belongs in history; refreshed data is authoritative.
- Multiple rapid Back/Forward commands must have stale-attempt protection consistent with Workspace generation handling.
- Do not add confirmation dialogs to history traversal.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Back and Forward restore destinations in another Workspace without confirmation.
- [ ] Successful traversal restores Workspace, Issue List View, Issue Search, and optional selected Issue together.
- [ ] Issue data is re-resolved from the destination Workspace rather than stored in history.
- [ ] A deleted destination Issue renders Issue-not-found while preserving the historical route.
- [ ] A failed Workspace traversal leaves the prior Workspace, route, Issue Explorer snapshot, and navigation boundaries intact.
- [ ] Failures raise the dismissible global error banner.
- [ ] Rollback does not create duplicate entries or recursive traversal.
- [ ] Rapid or stale traversal completions cannot publish a mismatched Workspace/Issue snapshot.
- [ ] Back/Forward controls and destination tooltips remain accurate after cross-Workspace success and failure.

## Test Plan

- Add coordinator tests for asynchronous cross-Workspace success, failure rollback, missing Issues, removed catalog entries, rapid traversal, stale completions, index integrity, and forward truncation after traversal.
- Add application integration tests with two Workspaces containing the same Issue ID but distinct data to prove correct re-resolution.
- Extend real-desktop atomic-switch acceptance with a Back/Forward round trip and a failed historical destination.
- Run Rust tests, renderer tests, transition-gate tests, TypeScript build, Ultracite, Tauri build, and available desktop acceptance tests.
title: Traverse Issue Navigation Entries across Workspaces
